 {"id":"bd-kro","title":"Implement full COW block rewrite path with allocation","description":"# Implement full COW block rewrite path with allocation

## GOAL
Complete the COW block rewrite path so writes allocate new blocks instead of overwriting, enabling true version chain semantics.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md: "COW block rewrite path: 🟡 Basic version copy only"

Current state: Versions are created but may reuse same block location.
Required: Writes MUST allocate new blocks, old blocks retained for active snapshots.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. COW Write Flow
```rust
impl MvccStore {
    pub fn write_cow(
        &self,
        block: BlockNumber,
        data: &[u8],
        txn: &Transaction,
        allocator: &dyn BlockAllocator,
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<BlockNumber> {
        // 1. Check if block has active snapshot references
        // 2. If yes: allocate new block, write there
        // 3. If no: could rewrite in place (optimization)
        // 4. Add to transaction write set with new location
        // 5. Return new block number
    }
}
```

### 2. Logical-to-Physical Mapping
- Logical block numbers stable across versions
- Physical locations vary (new allocation per write)
- Extent trees must be updated with new physical locations

### 3. Free Space Tracking
```rust
pub trait CowAllocator {
    /// Allocate block for COW write
    fn alloc_cow(&self, hint: Option<BlockNumber>, cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Mark block as freeable when no references
    fn defer_free(&self, block: BlockNumber, commit_seq: CommitSeq);
    
    /// Actually free blocks older than watermark
    fn gc_free(&self, watermark: CommitSeq, cx: &Cx) -> usize;
}
```

### 4. GC Integration
- Blocks freed only when no snapshot references
- Watermark = oldest active snapshot
- Freed blocks returned to allocator

## TESTS
1. Unit: COW write allocates new block
2. Unit: Old block readable via old snapshot
3. Integration: 100 writes to same logical block create 100 physical blocks
4. Integration: GC frees blocks when no refs
5. Property: Block count = active versions + overhead

## LOGGING
- COW allocation (trace): logical, old_physical, new_physical
- Defer free (trace): block, commit_seq
- GC (debug): freed count, watermark

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Writes never overwrite blocks with active snapshot refs
2. Logical block numbers stable
3. GC reclaims space correctly
4.
